channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Vance resigns from Senate as he and Trump prepare to take office
item.description: Vice President-elect JD Vance resigns from the U.S. Senate as he and President-elect Donald Trump prepare to take office on Jan. 20, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michelle Obama 'sends her thoughts and prayers' while absent from Carter's funeral
item.description: Michelle Obama wasn't among the mourners attending former President Carter's funeral service in Washington, but she says she's thinking of and praying for "everyone who loved and learned" from the late commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Trump hosts Republican governors in Florida and talks anew about Greenland and drones
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump hosted 22 of the nation's 27 Republican governors for dinner at his Florida club on Thursday night, looking to help drive the agenda in conservative-run states after he takes office in 11 days. Montana Gov.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Appeals court denies bid to block public release of special counsel's report on Trump Jan. 6 probe
item.description: A federal appeals court has denied a bid to block the public release of special counsel Jack Smith’s report on President-elect Donald Trump’s efforts to overturn his 2020 election loss.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: A divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s bid to delay his sentencing in his hush money case in New York.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court refuses Trump’s request to block Friday’s criminal sentencing
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 decision on Thursday refused President-elect Trump’s request to halt his criminal sentencing, now just hours away, closing off Trump’s last remaining pathway to avoid receiving his punishment for his 34-count felony conviction in New York.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court denies Trump attempt to stop sentencing in New York v. Trump
item.description: The United States Supreme Court has denied President-elect Trump's petition to block his Friday sentencing in New York v. Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vice President-elect JD Vance resigns from the Senate
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — Vice President-elect JD Vance is resigning from his seat in the U.S. Senate, effective Friday. Vance made his intentions known in a letter Thursday to Ohio Republican Gov. Mike DeWine, who will choose his successor.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic Sen. John Fetterman to meet with Trump at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pennsylvania Sen. John Fetterman will become the chamber's first Democrat to meet with President-elect Donald Trump since the election and plans to travel to Trump's private Mar-a-Lago resort in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris, Emhoff appear to ignore Trump, Obama as outgoing veep grimaces at presidential banter
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris and President-elect Donald Trump apparently did not acknowledge each other at the Washington National Cathedral during Jimmy Carter's state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some notable quotes from Jimmy Carter's funeral
item.description: Jimmy Carter was remembered at his funeral Thursday at Washington National Cathedral as a man of “character” and “something of a miracle” with “prophetic” vision.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says Newsom should resign as Los Angeles wildfires rage
item.description: The president-elect's criticism of Newsom has sparked a political battle over the fires that have killed at least five people and are far from contained.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: US orange juice and steel will be on Canada's list for retaliation if Trump imposes tariffs
item.description: A senior official says Canada is looking at putting retaliatory tariffs on American orange juice, sinks, toilets and some steel products if U.S. President-elect Donald Trump goes ahead with his threat to impose 25% on all Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: Rob Gillies, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden eulogizes ‘great American’ Jimmy Carter
item.description: President Biden on Thursday eulogized former President Carter, hailing the 39th president as a man of character who left an enduring legacy both during his time in the White House and after leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A contentious push to wrap up the 9/11 prosecutions takes an emotional toll on victims' families
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — After his only child was killed on 9/11, Ken Fairben looked for justice in a far-off military courtroom on the Guantánamo Bay naval base in Cuba. He traveled there multiple times to observe hearings for accused Sept.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lack of federal funds may mean no EBT replacements for scam victims
item.description: Many parents have reported benefits being erased from EBT cards in the weeks since Congress declined to continue funding.
item.dc:creator: Sean C. Davis, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio Supreme Court hears arguments in watchdog group's records dispute with state attorney general
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — The Ohio Supreme Court heard oral arguments Wednesday in a long-running public records case pitting the state’s top law enforcement officer against a national watchdog group that is digging into his ties with the Republican Attorneys General Association.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democratic senator: Alito has made impropriety the norm
item.description: Sen. Richard Blumenthal (D-Conn.), a member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, on Thursday blasted conservative Justice Samuel Alito for having a conversation with President-elect Trump amidst Trump’s effort to have courts halt his criminal sentencing in New York.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden cancels trip to Italy, meant as final foreign visit of presidency, as fires rage in California
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden canceled the final overseas trip of his presidency just hours before he was set to depart for Rome and the Vatican, choosing to remain in Washington to monitor the response to devastating fires raging in California.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats face early test with vote on Laken Riley Act
item.description: Democrats will face an early test on how to navigate immigration under President-elect Trump on Thursday when the Laken Riley Act becomes the first bill to hit the Senate floor.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Democrats warn party must look beyond 'blue wall' to win
item.description: When Democrats lost the White House in November and watched the Senate flip and the House remain in Republican control, they immediately set their sights on the next fight in 2028. But some Democrats say that's a mistake.
item.dc:creator: Amie Parnes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Immigration firebrand takes reins of the Hispanic Caucus
item.description: Latino Democrats, under new management for the 119th Congress, are looking to flex their muscle under a razor-thin Republican House majority that could make or break President-elect Trump’s immigration agenda. Last week, Rep. Adriano Espaillat (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What's next for Canada? 5 questions will help decide the future after Trudeau
item.description: "We're in a unique situation in Canadian politics," Shachi Kurl, president of the Angus Reid Institute, tells NPR. Voters need to replace a lame-duck leader — and cope with threats from Trump.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Jimmy Carter remembered by colleagues, family and friends as a devoted public servant
item.description: President Biden delivered a eulogy to former President Jimmy Carter at the National Cathedral service, noting the 39th president's faith and dedication to service.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: First Jan. 6 felony defendant allowed to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: U.S. District Judge Rudolph Contreras granted William Pope's motion despite objections from the Department of Justice.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Senate approves committee assignments, tees up confirmation hearings next week
item.description: The Senate on Tuesday approved its organizing resolution for the 119th Congress, officially setting the composition of committees and greenlighting nomination hearings to take place in the coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: These Democrats flipped their votes on the Laken Riley Act
item.description: Seven House Democrats flipped their votes to "yes" on a GOP-led bill named after the slain Georgia student Laken Riley that would require detention of migrants arrested for theft.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: President-elect Trump asks Supreme Court to block sentencing in hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is asking the Supreme Court to call off Friday's sentencing in his hush money case in New York. Trump's lawyers turned to the nation's highest court on Wednesday after New York courts refused to postpone the sentencing by Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Colleges prepare for new legal and political terrain under Trump
item.description: Colleges and universities are preparing for new legal and political territory as President-elect Trump returns to office. Concerns for international students, GOP attacks on higher education and increased protests on campus are just some of the worries on administrators' minds.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Schools around the US are weighing responses to possible immigration raids
item.description: Schools around the country are reviewing what to do if immigration agents come knocking as President-elect Donald Trump prepares to take office after campaigning on a pledge to deport millions of people.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump won't commit to not using military to get Panama Canal
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to commit to not using the U.S. military to gain control of the Panama Canal, after vowing last month to take over the operation of the key passageway.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about Trump and his keen interest in Greenland
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has said multiple times that the U.S. should buy Greenland, an autonomous territory of Denmark. The sparsely populated island is geopolitically important and mineral-rich.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Ex-President Jimmy Carter's body arrives in Washington
item.description: The next phase of former President Jimmy Carter's state funeral has begun as his body is transported to Washington.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: U.K. leader Starmer slams ‘lies and misinformation’ after attacks from Elon Musk
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday condemned "lies and misinformation" that he said are undermining U.K. democracy, in response to a barrage of attacks on his government from Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Rudy Giuliani is held in contempt of court in $148 million defamation case
item.description: Rudy Giuliani was found in contempt of court for failing to properly respond to requests for information as he turned over assets to satisfy a defamation ruling granted to two Georgia poll workers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management will get new interim director
item.description: The interim director of the Minnesota Office of Cannabis Management, who has led the office since it was created, is stepping down.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: GOP sues over timing of special election crucial to Minnesota House power balance
item.description: A lawsuit challenges a decision by Gov. Tim Walz to schedule a Jan. 28 special election after a November winner was deemed ineligible to take office in the House on Jan. 14.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News
